title: Microscopic Observational Relativity (MOR) / 微观观测相对论：基于多维投影结构的量子实在性重构
description: Microscopic Observational Relativity (MOR) is an ontological interpretational framework for quantum mechanics, independently developed by a non-professional researcher based on physical intuition. The core claim: micro-reality is relational, projective, and non-globally visible; observation factualises one cross-section (Sigma_M) of the complete projective structure (Omega_Q) within a specific observational reference frame M. The theory maintains a weak version (compatible with standard QM, Born rule preserved) and envisions a strong version with constrained deviation terms delta_i^MOR. This repository archives the complete version history from the initial MPQT draft (2025-05) through MOR v4.1 (2026-06) and the v5.0 strong-version launch draft.
notes: This work was independently conceived without prior systematic study of the quantum foundations literature. Post-hoc comparison reveals partial independent overlap with Relational Quantum Mechanics (Rovelli 1996), quantum Darwinism (Zurek 2003), and spacetime-from-entanglement research (Van Raamsdonk 2010). The repository preserves all historical versions unmodified to document the intellectual evolution of an independent researcher.
